method: ADR 0009 D5 — record the Linux CI baseline FIRST; ceilings are baseline p95 plus fixed headroom, set once per roadmap cycle, never ratcheted per item. Scenarios without maxP95Ms are in RECORDING mode: the bench reports them and cannot fail on them. A targetP95Ms records a preregistered target but does not arm enforcement until maxP95Ms is set from comparable evidence.

runner.measurement: fresh child processes; wall time of the complete spawn (node startup + TypeScript load + validate); cold and one-shot-warm doctor use identical argv/tree/cache state, with one discarded prime before warm samples; --no-cache is retained as an explicit legacy flag while no Ark cache exists; non-zero child exits abort the run

hook.rationale: The per-keystroke interactive path: ark-mcp --hook validates the single proposed file (no scan-cache involvement — one distribution). Phase Y's 683.761 ms baseline remains the comparison point for Z07. Phase Z source 6fa5079 CI run 29649631288 measured 591.165 ms and 595.053 ms on unchanged-head attempts; the frozen 900 ms ceiling retains 51.25% headroom over the worse Phase Z observation and is never ratcheted per item (ADR 0009 D5).

doctorCold.rationale: Cold one-shot doctor over the tree. Phase Y's 5,154.522 ms baseline remains the retained comparison point. Phase Z source 6fa5079 CI run 29649631288 measured 5,072.093 ms and 4,799.834 ms on unchanged-head attempts; the frozen 6,800 ms ceiling retains 34.06% headroom over the worse Phase Z observation and is never ratcheted per item (ADR 0009 D5).

doctorOneShotWarm.rationale: Z07 recording lane: fresh doctor processes after one discarded prime, using the exact cold argv and immutable fixture/cache state. This establishes the like-for-like one-shot warm baseline before a resident implementation; it is not a resident or 10x claim.

doctorResidentWarm.rationale: Z07 preregistered absolute UX target. The metric remains unavailable and unenforced until a resident pilot reports exact cold/warm JSON, hash, verdict, edit/delete, and invalidation parity.
